item.title: Anybody Enjoy Some MTG?
item.description:
The nerd you bullied for playing MTG back in high school looks like this now btw... Also I am back on Instagram (Yay?)! Thank you all for supporting me and making a stink about it, folks heard y'all and gave me back the account they wrongfully suspended. https://www.instagram.com/alsoashleyb/
My 10th Doctor Commander Deck crushed it last night!
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Meme Redistribution Centre TM
item.description: At the vets. Get the results tomorrow: Kyrie and I need your help! If you've ever found solace or joy in the Meme Stream (or any other content I post) please consider donating. Even $5 or $10 makes a massive difference to me. And it might save Kyrie's life! For 3 years Kyrie has been getting by using pain relief to cope with suspected hip displasia because I didn't have the money for medical investigation and treatment. But now she's reached the point of no return. While attempting to run at the dog park yesterday she did ~something~ to herself and suddenly dropped to the ground screaming her head off - as only huskies can. (It caused quite a scene) I raced over to her suspecting she'd cut her foot or something, but she has no external injuries. She couldn't walk enough to get home, and my car died in February, and I can't afford to fix OR replace it. So I had to ask a kind stranger at the dog park if she could drive us home. I then immediately called the vets to see how much investigative x-rays are now (they quoted ($823 - $923) and asked if they did payment plans. They don't, but they put me on to VetPay to a apply for finance, which I did. I was denied finance though because I live on a disability pension and already go without food or a cent to my name every second week between payments. So I obviously couldn't afford 10k finance. (It was with a try ¯⁠\⁠_⁠(⁠ツ⁠)⁠_⁠/⁠¯ ) And so now I'm asking you, friends, to contribute (if you can) to help fund Kyrie's initial investigation and subsequent required treatment. She can't walk at all now, even on CBD, Meloxicam, predisilone, and Panadol. If I can't fund getting her back on her feet my only other option will be putting her down ($700) which would be a crying shame because she is so young and wants to live. Even this morning she tried to come for a walk with us - when she can barely even stand. I've included the GoFundMe link below, and as soon as I have the first $1000 of take her for the investigative x-rays. Mahalo (and awooo!) https://www.gofundme.com/f/valkyrie-urgently-needs-hip-surgery
item.dc:creator: HuskyHairExtraordinaire

item.title: 1200 DETAINEES FROM ALLIGATOR ALCATRAZ ARE MISSING.
item.description:
https://www.irishlegal.com/articles/us-hundreds-of-detainees-vanish-from-floridas-alligator-alcatraz
https://www.miamiherald.com/news/local/immigration/article312042943.html
https://www.miamiherald.com/news/local/immigration/article312042943.html
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lgKGIhfKrpY
The “desaparecidos” crime refers to the forced disappearance of people by state or paramilitary actors, often to silence dissent or instill fear. Victims are abducted, detained secretly, and never seen again, with authorities denying responsibility. It’s considered a grave human rights violation, leaving families without information and preventing legal recourse.
item.dc:creator: Dr3sd4

item.title: It was one long flight of stairs
item.dc:creator: ChocolatePoopy

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1485 (stakeout)
item.description:
Let's get this bread, I guess.
I wrap up this post while on a takeout for a bird that has no business being in the artificial wetlands complex outside Sedona: a gull from the far Arctic, hopelessly lost.
Kind of how i'm feeling, overall, really.
It's a juvenile Sabine's Gull. I saw one once before, in college on a lake near Flagstaff, back when life was so much easier, and back when the world seemed less fucked up.
23 years later, with my life mostly in shambles, I seek it to try and find some semblance of order and symmetry,some sort of sign that everything will become okay again and that I won't have to worry about losing everything.
It was seen here yesterday, along with some Caspian Terns.
While fall has definitely arrived here today, with the first of season White-crowned Sparrows returning here from their sojourn from either Utah mountains or Canadian Rockies....
The only whiteish-grey bird I'm seeing today right now is the first of season Eared Grebe thar keeps fucking with me out on the horizon. Well, and occasionally Mallard butts as they dip down into the water.
Once again, I proved that I rarely, if ever, make the right decisions.
Yesterday's bird omens on the mountain were Hella strange too: a flock of five Mexican Jays were flying south, calling. I've never seen them on Mingus Mountain before, and to the best of my knowledge, nobody else has either.
@bearstrap asked yesterday if I was a cryptid after a lore-dropping comment in another post-yeah, basically.
Where's my little lost gull to help me feel whole again, universe?
ANYWAY I also saw three Lewis's Woodpeckers at another part of the mountain yesterday which was equally strange.
They're also very handsome woodpeckers: pink and dark iridescent green. Look that shit up. They're wild.
I don't know how to interpret these as omens, but it felt like yesterday everything was going to be beautiful and okay,and then i saw the posts on eBird about the Sabine's Gull and got even more excited. I'd love to see one again. They're such fabulous birds. So i decided I'd make a go of it today.
But then I woke up feeling this incredible, overwhelming, oppressive sense of doom. DOOM. with all capital letters.
Doom doom doom went the drums in the deep of my mind. I almost didn't come here.
Ah well. Rare bird stakeouts are always a crap shoot, but i could have used the morale boost. If I can't find a job or figure out this disability stuff, Pippin and I will be on the street in about 3 months.
This damned Eared Grebe is also frustrating me because it keeps diving when I try to get a photo. Bitch, sit still.
I know exactly where this sign is. I've been here. This is East of Sedona. Got some great photos of Common Black-hawks there.
There's a corner of the pond i cant see, but no trespassing behind the fence. Im not so sure it would be there, either.
These killdeer are also a bit bothery, I see them flying and think for a split second they're the terns or the gull. But no dice.
Anyway. Enough rambling. My battery is almost dead on my phone, ive gotta post this now.
Pippin tax Today's message from Pippin (asked before i left at dawn): "I don't know" ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Lynching Project Update
item.description:
What you see here is a heat map of all racially-motivated lynchings of black citizens that I have so far logged. Now that the image has your attention, I can tell you what this is. Since early October 2024 I have been working on a database of known and named black victims of racially-motivated violence from 1900 to 1950. Most often this takes the form of lynchings, but there are times when it can be overtly "legal." So, as I approach the one year anniversary, I thought I might give an update. Since I'm not exactly known for my skills with graphics software, I'm afraid much of this will simply be screenshots of snippets of the project from the various sheets I have going in Excel.
Luckily, Excel can do maps! This is a county-level view. I'm working on a spreadsheet that will be able to drill down to state level on a click, which brings us to...
... the state level! Georgia is used here as an example. Why Georgia? Because it's the one I had active, of course!
As I'm recording years, I thought a line graph with some notable events might be helpful to really visualize the numbers. Notice the spike in 1919. The Red Summer largely targeted returning soldiers who had the nerve to want to be made equals, and the sheer audacity to wear a uniform. There's also a spike at the start of the Great Depression, which I imagine is due to the sudden economic turn. That jump in 1906 though? I have no idea what that is other than possibly just an anomaly due to reporting.
As I go more in-depth, I realized I can generate very precise lists to illustrate exatctly how dangerous some areas could be over that 50 year span. It cuts some off as I have it arranged now, but that's Louisiana, Illinois, and Georgia in the TOP 3 COUNTIES section.
So why not do a yearly percentage? It goes past 1924, I promise, I just didn't screenshot the entire thing. Again we see that jump in 1906.
So, last of all we have my goals. It took almost one year to get 1,515 names done with around 100 additional names being researched and removed. I still have a long ways to go. So, would you like some additional non-picture information? I've also contacted around 20 libraries! 18 have replied, and they've been amazing help. Well, except one. They know who they are. I've contacted at least 6 churches, but I've never once had one reply. I've even been in contact with two families. That was something. Anyway, here's the link! https://umap.openstreetmap.fr/en/map/racially-motivated-lynchings-1900-1950_1131763#3/34.71/-92.02
item.dc:creator: Luriden

item.title: Today is remember me Thursday
item.description: Every shelter pet deserves a chance at love. On Remember Me Thursday, let’s shine a light on adoption and help pets find their forever homes.

item.title: To my coworker who brags about working OT for free
item.description: A. We are salaried, we don’t get any OT without the 7 gemstones, 5 foreskins, and a blood sacrifice (management approval). B. You’re a dense MF who thinks management is your friend because you got a $100 dollar Amazon gift card last quarter. (I changed the bonus type for this bit but they did get something small) C. You’re the reason why we can’t have nice things, LIKE A UNION!!!

item.title: Cardboard oil painting! [OC]
item.description: As it turns out, Van Gogh also used cardboard for his oil paintings!! A cereal box is as much of a canvas as anything else ;D
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: Arrival…
item.description: An arribada is the mass nesting event of sea turtles (especially olive ridley turtles) where thousands of them come ashore at once to lay eggs. The word comes from Spanish, meaning “arrival.” It’s one of the most spectacular wildlife events on Earth — beaches in places like Odisha (India) and Costa Rica are famous for it.
item.dc:creator: tractorist
